title: Tech
description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from Tech, skipping any feeds with errors.

items.title: Bug in student admissions website exposed children’s personal information
items.description: Ravenna Hub, which lets parents apply and track the status of their kids' applications across thousands of schools, allowed any logged-in user to acce...

items.title: Current is a new RSS reader that’s more like a river than an inbox
items.description: Current introduces a stress-free, totally reimagined RSS news reading app as a one-time paid download.

items.title: The boys’ club no one was supposed to write about
items.description: Reporter Zoë Bernard spent months talking to 51 people (31 of them gay men) to map out a subculture that's been an open secret in Silicon Valley for y...

items.title: Toyota hires seven Agility humanoid robots for Canadian factory
items.description: The robots will be unloading totes full of auto parts from an automated warehouse tugger.

items.title: Reload wants to give your AI agents a shared memory
items.description: Reload announces a $2.275 million raise in a round led by Anthemis and the launch of its first AI employee, Epic.

items.title: YouTube’s latest experiment brings its conversational AI tool to TVs
items.description: YouTube is testing conversational AI on smart TVs, allowing viewers to ask the assistant questions related to the video they're watching on the big sc...

items.title: Sex toys maker Tenga says hacker stole customer information
items.description: The Japanese sex toy maker said a hacker broke into an employee's inbox and stole customer names, email addresses, and correspondence, including order...

items.title: SpaceX’s Starbase city is getting its own court
items.description: Elon Musk's company town already has a volunteer fire department, and is forming a Starbase Police Department. Now it wants its own court, too.

items.title: Why these startup CEOs don’t think AI will replace human roles
items.description: The CEOs of Read AI and Lucidya told TechCrunch at Web Summit Qatar that they see AI tools replacing tasks, rather than workers.

items.title: OpenAI reportedly finalizing $100B deal at more than $850B valuation
